Package trienodes

Class ACTrieNode

java.lang.Object
trienodes.ACTrieNode
Direct Known Subclasses:
UkkonenTrieNode
public class ACTrieNode
extends java.lang.Object
The basic Aho-Corasick supporting Trie Node - It keeps track of all transitions, failure paths and can be used to create a DFA.

Note on the implementation: The goto function (successorNodes) maps characters to integers to ACTrieNodes. We do this to make use of fast array access in small alphabets. It scales badly for large alphabets because we need to keep a list the size of the alphabet with each node. It would be more efficient to switch to an implementation based on HashMap[Character, Integer] in such cases.

Since:
26.12.2020, Sa.
Author:
Markus Walder

    Modifier and Type Method Description
    java.util.List<ACTrieNode> getAllPossibleDFATransitions()
    Returns all reachable nodes of the DFA node.
    ACTrieNode getDFATransition​(char input)
    Returns the next node in the constructed Aho-Corasick DFA, note that this is different to getNextNode which returns the next node in the Trie.

    Gets the first node on the failure path starting from this node.

    Returns the next node in the Aho-Corasick String Matcher Trie, note that this is different to getDFATransition.

    Utility method that returns all (even null) successors in the AC-Trie.
    boolean isLeafInAhoCorasickGraph()

  • Constructor Details

    • ACTrieNode

      protected ACTrieNode​(LanguageParameter parameters, boolean isEndOfWord, char pch)

  • Method Details

    • getNextNode

      public ACTrieNode getNextNode​(char input)
      Returns the next node in the Aho-Corasick String Matcher Trie, note that this is different to getDFATransition. It will return null, if there is no node reachable using input. This function does not handle any transitions to the failure node.
      Parameters:
      input - Character used to determine next node
      Returns:
      ACTrieNode the node reachable using input or null if there is no reachable node

    • setNextNode

      public void setNextNode​(char input, ACTrieNode node)

    • getFail

      public ACTrieNode getFail()
      Gets the first node on the failure path starting from this node. This node is the node representing the longest prefix string matching the suffix of the string represented by our current node.
      Returns:
      ACTrieNode the fail node

    • setFail

      public void setFail​(ACTrieNode fail)

    • getSuccessorNodes

      public java.util.List<ACTrieNode> getSuccessorNodes()
      Utility method that returns all (even null) successors in the AC-Trie.
      Returns:
      List[ACTrieNode] a list of all successor tree nodes

    • getDFATransition

      public ACTrieNode getDFATransition​(char input)
      Returns the next node in the constructed Aho-Corasick DFA, note that this is different to getNextNode which returns the next node in the Trie. The DFA is more efficient at matching strings because it combines the transition and failure function.
      Parameters:
      input - Character used to determine next node
      Returns:
      ACTrieNode the node reachable using input or null if there is no reachable node

    • setDFATransition

      public void setDFATransition​(char input, ACTrieNode node)

    • getAllPossibleDFATransitions

      public java.util.List<ACTrieNode> getAllPossibleDFATransitions()
      Returns all reachable nodes of the DFA node.
      Returns:
      List[ACTrieNode] the nodes reachable from this node

    • isLeafInAhoCorasickGraph

      public boolean isLeafInAhoCorasickGraph()
      Utility method that returns whether the node is a leaf in the Aho-Corasick Trie.
      Returns:
      Boolean - true if node is leaf in AC Graph
